title: Remote Code Execution in log4j (CVE-2021-44228)
description: A critical RCE vulnerability in Apache Log4j 2.x allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted log messages using JNDI lookup patterns.
plain_description: Your app uses a logging library with a known backdoor — an attacker sending a specific string in a user-submitted field can run code on your server. Upgrade log4j-core to 2.17.1 or later.
why_this_matters: Public exploit available. Internet-facing service processes untrusted input. Log4j 2.14.1 confirmed in the dependency tree.

title: Prototype Pollution in lodash
description: lodash before 4.17.21 is vulnerable to prototype pollution via the set function when used with user-controlled paths.
plain_description: A helper library you rely on lets attackers tamper with shared JavaScript objects, which can lead to privilege escalation or denial of service. Bump lodash to 4.17.21.
why_this_matters: Reached from the /api/invoice endpoint which accepts untrusted user input. Upgrade path is clean — a minor version bump.

title: Public-read S3 bucket policy on user uploads bucket
description: The S3 bucket `prod-user-uploads` has a bucket policy granting s3:GetObject to `*` (any principal). This exposes all uploaded files to the public internet.
plain_description: A storage bucket that's supposed to be private is set to world-readable. Anyone on the internet can list and download user-uploaded files. Remove the public-read grant and use signed URLs instead.
why_this_matters: Customer PII confirmed in the bucket. Fix requires Terraform change + signed-URL migration for the frontend. Work started 2026-04-14.

title: Stripe live API key committed to frontend repo
description: A Stripe secret key matching `sk_live_*` was committed in `frontend/src/config/payments.ts` at commit `a7f3c2e`. Key is still active per Stripe API check.
plain_description: A live payment API key was pushed to the public repo three days ago. It's still valid, which means anyone who cloned the repo can charge cards on your Stripe account. Rotate the key now, then purge it from git history.
why_this_matters: Key rotated 2026-04-11 at 17:04 UTC. Old key revoked. Awaiting validation that no fraudulent charges were made in the exposure window.

title: OpenSSL DoS in DH key generation (CVE-2023-5678)
description: OpenSSL versions prior to 3.0.12 are vulnerable to DoS when processing excessively large Diffie-Hellman key generation requests.
plain_description: The TLS library inside your container can be crashed by an attacker sending an oversized cryptographic request. Rebuild the container from a base image with OpenSSL 3.0.12 or later.
why_this_matters: Edge-facing TLS terminator. DoS would take the full ingress path down. Base image pinned at Ubuntu 22.04 with OpenSSL 3.0.2 — needs a rebuild.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery in axios
description: axios versions prior to 1.6.0 allow SSRF when making requests with user-controlled URLs, enabling access to internal-only services.
plain_description: Your HTTP client lets attackers pivot requests to internal-only systems (like cloud metadata endpoints) if it's ever called with a URL derived from user input. Upgrade axios to 1.6.0.
why_this_matters: Webhook service fetches user-supplied URLs for content previews — textbook SSRF sink. Upgrade blocked by breaking change in axios 1.x response interceptors.

title: Proxy-Authorization header leak in requests
description: python-requests versions prior to 2.32.0 may leak the Proxy-Authorization header when following redirects across hosts.
plain_description: Your Python HTTP library can accidentally send proxy credentials to the wrong server when a request gets redirected. Upgrade requests to 2.32.0 or later.
why_this_matters: Only exploitable if the service is configured with a proxy (we are, for outbound S3). Fix in an open PR — pending data-platform review.

title: Missing HSTS header on marketing site
description: The response from marketing.acme.com does not include a Strict-Transport-Security header. HTTPS downgrade attacks are possible for first-time visitors.
plain_description: Your marketing site works over HTTPS but doesn't tell browsers to insist on HTTPS for future visits. A network attacker could downgrade a first-time visitor to plain HTTP. Add the HSTS header at the edge.
why_this_matters: Low severity — marketing site hosts no user data. Fix is a one-line CDN config change.

title: Regex DoS in d3-color
description: d3-color versions prior to 3.1.0 are vulnerable to regex denial of service via the rgb() parser with specially crafted input.
plain_description: A charting library can be made to hang if it tries to parse a maliciously crafted color string. Fix was a minor-version bump; already merged and deployed.
why_this_matters: Closed loop — rescanned by Snyk on 2026-04-13 and finding no longer present. Keeping in history for audit trail.

title: gzip arbitrary file write in xzgrep (CVE-2022-1271)
description: xzgrep on Debian Bullseye base images could be coerced into writing to arbitrary paths via crafted filenames. Exploitable only when xzgrep is run on attacker-controlled input.
plain_description: A base image utility has a path-traversal bug, but it's only exploitable if you run it on untrusted filenames — which we don't. Rebuilt the container to suppress the scanner noise.
why_this_matters: Not reachable in our configuration (we never invoke xzgrep on user input). Closed with exception documented in ticket PLAT-4412.
